title: Developing a hermes agent to turn a profit (This is more difficult than I thought)
author: getstonkzilla
selftext:
**Original post**: [https://www.reddit.com/r/ai\_trading/comments/1t0rghe/developing\_a\_hermes\_agent\_to\_turn\_a\_profit/](https://www.reddit.com/r/ai_trading/comments/1t0rghe/developing_a_hermes_agent_to_turn_a_profit/)

**TLDR**: Week 1 — we're in the green but s&p put up a fight and won. Fees ate over 50% of gains. Biggest lesson - raw data setups are arbed out, the edge has to come from interpretation.

**Week 1 Review**

Yes I know; before you say "yeh no shit", here I was thinking this was going to be a lot simpler than it has been. This bot is evolving fast (for good and worse) and keeping up is proving difficult.

**Key takeaway from week 1**

Data is great, but most setups are arbed out — what I mean is, you and every other quant is looking at the same data. Interpretation of this data is now where this project has led, and trying to develop some sort of edge.

TARS has evolved. What started as a simple value scan bot (which to be fair it sort of still is), is now asking for options to hedge its plays, as well as metadata tracking. I did prompt it to better understand bayesian methods ([https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bayesian\_statistics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bayesian_statistics)); and it is quite excited to develop this further. Unfortunately (still quite a lot of development needed as well) we won't see dividends in this for a few months while it collects at least 90 days of market context — this is fine, the idea is it will get stronger over time.

**Patching, patching, more patching** — trades that were taken last week likely would not have been taken if the patches were applied sooner. Check out [https://www.stonkzilla.com/terminal](https://www.stonkzilla.com/terminal) for the complete log.

I now have a scripts section on the web terminal to show the current toolkit and interaction with the db.

**Fees and limit orders** — shout out to u/Spartacus_Nakamoto for provoking this. As I can see already, fees account for over 50% of the gains we made this week. And a simple mistake in the beginning gave us our first loss, being an over trade.

We are in the green, but the S&P proved a better bet, however we can make this work.

**What I need:** Experienced traders, other devs, bot creators — I want constructive comments, **share this around** and get involved. Tear it apart (respectfully, and tell me what's wrong with it, what's right with it). If you don't want to comment, shoot me a message; however if it is just spam or other shit, I won't reply.
